description:
Load the high-resolution crystal structure of a mutant collagen peptide (PDB: 1CAG) that models Osteogenesis Imperfecta. The mutation is a Gly-to-Ala substitution at position 15 in the central axis of the triple helix. Your task: 
1. Identify the three chains (A, B, C) and locate the central mutation site (Ala 15) on each chain.
2. Measure the steric clash distance from the C-beta (CB) atom of Ala 15 on Chain A to the nearest backbone atom of an adjacent chain (Chain B or C).
3. Create a publication-quality visualization showing the collagen triple helix with the three Ala 15 residues explicitly highlighted (e.g., as spheres or brightly colored sticks) to demonstrate how their side chains disrupt the crowded core.
4. Save the figure as a PNG to ~/PyMOL_Data/images/collagen_mutation.png.
5. Write a plain-text report to ~/PyMOL_Data/collagen_clash_report.txt documenting: the identity of the mutant residue (Ala 15), the measured steric clash distance in Angstroms, and a brief explanation of how this substitution disrupts the collagen fold.
